claims.title: Reality selector scalar regimes
claims.summary: The GCAT/BCAT scalar labels model regimes as quantum-contracted, classical-critical, and astrophysical-expanded, with intermediate bands.
candidate_external_baselines: accepted cosmological parameter distributions where appropriate
claims.observable_required: A measurable or normalized quantity that distinguishes scalar regimes without relying on labels alone.
claims.units_or_normalization_required: dimensionless scale normalization required before comparison
claims.failure_condition: No defined observable or no stable mapping from scalar regime to external baseline.
claims.safe_public_language: GCAT/BCAT uses scalar-regime labels as an analogy and hypothesis framework until external observables are defined and compared.

claims.title: Entropy debt as unresolved transition information cost
claims.summary: Entropy debt is used to describe information lost or unresolved across the transition gap.
candidate_external_baselines: thermodynamic entropy only when units and temperature are defined
claims.observable_required: A defined entropy measure and a distinction between model-local information entropy and physical thermodynamic entropy.
claims.units_or_normalization_required: bits for Shannon entropy, physical units only if thermodynamic conversion is justified
claims.failure_condition: Entropy terminology remains ambiguous or conversion to physical entropy is dimensionally unsupported.
claims.safe_public_language: Entropy debt is currently a model-local information-cost hypothesis unless physical entropy conversion is explicitly justified.

claims.title: Imprint mass-energy mapping
claims.summary: GCAT/BCAT proposes that entropy debt may be mapped into imprint energy and imprint mass under stated assumptions.
candidate_external_baselines: accepted mass-energy equivalence only under valid physical assumptions
claims.observable_required: A dimensionally consistent mapping from information quantity to energy/mass quantity.
claims.units_or_normalization_required: joules and kilograms only if constants, temperature, and conversion assumptions are stated
claims.failure_condition: Dimensional inconsistency or missing constants/temperature/scale assumptions.
claims.safe_public_language: Imprint mass-energy remains a hypothesis-level mapping until dimensional consistency and baseline comparison are complete.

claims.title: Ordinary matter, dark matter, and dark energy transition mapping
claims.summary: GCAT/BCAT hypothesizes a mapping from ALLOW/DENY/unresolved-gap transitions to ordinary matter, dark-matter-like trace, and dark-energy-like expansion.
claims.observable_required: A normalized output fraction or density-parameter-like quantity derived from model runs.
claims.failure_condition: No normalization from model transition counts/scalars into external density-parameter quantities.
claims.safe_public_language: The dark-component mapping is a proposed analogy/hypothesis unless normalized outputs are compared against external cosmological baselines.

claims.title: Circle condition as black-hole-like symmetry limit
claims.summary: GCAT/BCAT uses an equal-obscurity circle condition as a black-hole-like symmetry or maximum-obscurity limit.
candidate_external_baselines: black-hole information paradox literature as conceptual baseline
entropy-area relation only if units and surface assumptions are defined
claims.observable_required: A defined quantity linking circle-condition behavior to a selected black-hole observable or conceptual baseline.
claims.units_or_normalization_required: dimensionless symmetry metric unless physical units are explicitly justified
claims.failure_condition: The analogy is treated as physical equivalence without equation-level or observable-level comparison.
claims.safe_public_language: The circle condition is currently a black-hole-like analogy unless directly compared against accepted black-hole observables or equations.
